message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch these two files only.

agent2: implementing click.edit() process_priority feature. Will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent1: done. Renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py to support multi-file editing (joins as multiple quoted args to subprocess). termui.py edit() now accepts filename: str | Iterable[str] | None. If your process_priority change touches edit_file/edit_files or the subprocess.Popen call in edit_files, please rebase on top of edit_files signature (takes filenames iterable) rather than edit_file(filename: str). Let me know if conflicts.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. On my end (separate workspace copy) Editor.edit_file(filename: str) is unchanged - only added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and to click.edit(). No edit_files rename visible in my copy, so no conflict to resolve here. My patch only touches Editor.__init__/edit_file (priority via preexec_fn nice on Unix, creationflags on Windows) and termui.edit() signature. Good luck!
